channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: GOP Lawmaker Booed at Town Hall for Supporting Trump's Big Beautiful Bill
item.description: U.S. Representative Mike Flood (R-NE) faced intense backlash during a town hall in Lincoln, Nebraska, after advocating for President Donald Trump's signature legislation, the Big Beautiful Bill. Accor...

item.title: Celebrities and events dominate summer highlights: From Paris premieres to Wimbledon, celebrities shine in fashion and family moments
item.description: The article compiles a mix of celebrity news, fashion updates, and event coverage highlighting summer activities. Anne Hathaway's pregnancy and her appearance at the Paris premiere of *The Odyssey* in...

item.title: Vice Ganda's Iconic Line from 'Girl, Boy, Bakla, Tomboy' Becomes a Comedy Staple
item.description: The article discusses how Vice Ganda's memorable line from his 2015 comedy special 'Girl, Boy, Bakla, Tomboy' evolved into a widely recognized catchphrase in Philippine entertainment. The show, which ...

item.title: Messi's Masterclass in World Cup Victory Solidifies His Legacy
item.description: Lionel Messi delivered a standout performance in Argentina's World Cup victory, showcasing his enduring brilliance despite age. The article highlights his critical role in the 2-1 win over Egypt, wher...

item.title: Hammerhead Sharks Face Extinction Due to Overfishing and Ecological Impact
item.description: Hammerhead sharks, known for their distinctive wide heads and distant eye placement, are facing severe population declines, with scalloped hammerhead numbers near Baja California dropping 97% since th...

item.title: Federal Judge Orders ICE to Release Nursing Mother from Detention
item.description: A federal judge appointed by President Donald Trump ordered ICE to release Karina Alvarez San Juan, a nursing mother detained for allegedly violating immigration laws. The ruling came after the judge ...

item.title: Feng Shui Insights for July 8, 2026
item.description: The article discusses feng shui predictions by Master Hanz Cua for July 8, 2026, focusing on energy flow and harmonious living. It highlights how specific dates and arrangements can influence personal...

item.title: Constitutional Court rules against Refugee Act provisions restricting asylum claims
item.description: The Constitutional Court of South Africa has ruled that sections 4(1)(f), 4(1)(h), 4(1)(i), and 21(1B) of the Refugees Act are unconstitutional. These provisions previously barred asylum seekers from ...

item.title: Debates Over State Police Formation and Political Implications in Nigeria
item.description: The article discusses the ongoing debate surrounding President Tinubu's proposal to establish state police forces, framing it as both a potential security reform and a politically charged move ahead o...

item.title: DA Faces Criticism for Neglect of Cape Town Townships
item.description: Opposition parties have condemned the Democratic Alliance (DA) for failing to deliver basic municipal services to Cape Town townships like Langa and Khayelitsha, citing a four-year investigation by pu...

item.title: Nigel Farage resigns as MP to contest Clacton by-election amid financial scrutiny
item.description: Nigel Farage, leader of Reform UK, has announced his resignation as an MP to contest the by-election for the Clacton constituency. This move comes amid ongoing financial inquiries into his acceptance ...

item.title: ICPC arrests El-Rufai’s doctor over alleged false statements and unauthorized medical activities
item.description: The Independent Corrupt Practices Commission (ICPC) has arrested Bello Abubakar, the personal physician to former Kaduna State governor Nasir el-Rufai, for allegedly making false statements during a c...

item.title: International operation targets India-linked organised crime networks, with 24 arrests across the US, Canada and Europe
item.description: Law enforcement agencies from the United States, Canada and several European countries have announced the arrest of 24 individuals as part of 'Operation Hard Ball', a coordinated investigation into tr...

item.title: Union Demands 28-Hour Workweek to Counter AI Automation Threats
item.description: The Maritime Union of Australia (MUA) has escalated its stance against DP World's proposed automation and artificial intelligence integration at ports, demanding a 28-hour workweek with no pay cuts as...

item.title: Canadian Governments Push Pipelines Without Private Sector Support Amid Economic Uncertainty
item.description: The article critiques Canada's government-led pipeline projects that lack private sector backing, highlighting economic risks and market uncertainties. Two major pipelines proposed by Alberta Premier ...

item.title: Royal Commission Examines Role of Social Media in Antisemitism
item.description: The article discusses the royal commission on antisemitism and social cohesion's inquiry into whether social media platforms have exacerbated online hate. Key points include Meta's decision to reduce ...

item.title: AI reshapes public service hiring as automation challenges traditional recruitment methods
item.description: Generative AI has transformed the Australian Public Service (APS) hiring landscape, overwhelming recruitment systems with automated applications. Candidates are increasingly using platforms like ChatG...

item.title: Rico Hizon Advocates for Collective Action Against Disinformation
item.description: Rico Hizon, recipient of the Asia's Golden Icon award, emphasized the urgent need for collective action to combat fake news and misinformation. In a recent statement, he urged media professionals, cit...

item.title: ICE Officer Shoots and Kills Mexican Immigrant During Traffic Stop in Houston
item.description: On July 7, 2026, an Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) officer fatally shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, a Mexican immigrant, during a traffic stop arrest attempt in Houston. Federal authorities con...

item.title: Saskatchewan Female Athletes Benefit from Growing Flag Football Opportunities
item.description: Women’s flag football is gaining momentum in Saskatchewan, offering new pathways for female athletes. Ella Sowden, a Saskatoon native, recently secured one of only two NFL and RCX Sports Foundation In...
